title: xolisma villosa var. pubescens
intro: Xolisma villosa var. pubescens, also known as the hairy or woolly Xolisma, is a variety of the Xolisma villosa plant species, which is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The hairy Xolisma has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Herbal preparations of Xolisma villosa var. pubescens include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
